items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit 70b48842 (PR #58371) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.3 was released (triggered by the version-increment commit). The 1.0.0-beta.2 and 1.0.0-beta.3 releases added several user-facing behaviors — chat isolation enforcement, session ID header, metadata validation, and `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation — that are not documented in the README or troubleshooting section. ## Gaps Found - **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. This is a multi-tenancy security feature (added beta.2): when a response is created with this header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must include the same key; mismatched or missing keys return 404. Users implementing multi-tenant servers need to be aware of this behavior. - **`x-agent-session-id` response header** (added beta.2) is not documented. The library echoes the resolved session ID as a response header on all protocol endpoints; clients may need to forward or log this header. - **Metadata constraints** (added beta.2) are not documented: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 characters, value length ≤ 512 characters. Users passing metadata will receive 400 errors without knowing the limits unless they read the CHANGELOG. - **`previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` validation** (added beta.3): these values are now validated against the provider before the handler is invoked, returning 404/400 immediately. The Troubleshooting section does not list these new error cases. - **Troubleshooting section** is missing error entries for: isolation key mismatch → 404, invalid `previous_response_id` → 404, invalid `conversation.id` → 400, metadata constraint violation → 400.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit** **1a. Add a "Chat isolation" subsection under `## Key concepts`** (after the "Thread safety" section, before `## Examples`): ```markdown ### Chat isolation To enforce tenant isolation, clients may include an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header when creating a response. Once set, every subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems call for that response **must** include the same key. Calls with a mismatched or missing key return **404** (indistinguishable from not-found) to prevent cross-tenant information leakage. The isolation key value is never logged. ### Session ID header All protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems) echo the resolved session ID as the `x-agent-session-id` response header. The value corresponds to the `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable (or the deterministically derived session ID when a conversational context is available). Clients that need to correlate requests to a session can read this header from any response. ### Metadata constraints Metadata maps supplied in the request body are validated before the handler is invoked: - Maximum **16 keys** - Key length ≤ **64 characters** - Value length ≤ **512 characters** Requests that exceed these limits are rejected with **400 Bad Request** before your handler is called. ``` **1b. Expand the `### Common errors` subsection under `## Troubleshooting`** to add new entries: Add the following bullets to the existing list: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (conversation validation): `previous_response_id` was supplied but does not exist in the provider. Validation happens before your handler is invoked; the resolved history is cached for the request lifetime. - **400 Bad Request** (conversation validation): `conversation.id` was supplied but does not match the stored conversation record. - **400 Bad Request** (metadata): A metadata map exceeds 16 keys, or a key exceeds 64 characters, or a value exceeds 512 characters. - **404 Not Found** (isolation key): A GET, Cancel, DELETE, or InputItems request was made with a missing or mismatched `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` relative to the value used at creation time. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for this change. The documentation additions are conceptual/reference prose, not runnable sample code. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already has all required sections (Getting started → Key concepts → Examples → Troubleshooting → Next steps → Contributing). The edits above add subsections inside existing sections; no structural reorganization is needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm git diff shows no unintended changes to README snippet blocks 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; verify the API listing is unchanged 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AI Agent Server] Docs: README missing chat isolation, session ID header, metadata constraints, and updated validation behavior
